# Global rate limit configuration entry reference

This page provides reference information for rate limit configuration entries. Rate limits enable Consul operators to set global RPC caps, implement emergency throttling, exempt critical endpoints, and provide better observability across the entire cluster.

@include 'alerts/enterprise-alert.mdx'

## Configuration model

The following list outlines field hierarchy, language-specific data types, and requirements in the rate limit configuration entry. Click on a property name to view additional details, including default values.

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="HCL and JSON" group="hcl">

- [`Kind`](#kind): string | required | must be set to `rate-limit`
- [`Name`](#name): string | required
- [`Config`](#config): map | required
  - [`ReadRate`](#config-readrate): number | `100`
  - [`WriteRate`](#config-writerate): number | `100`
  - [`Priority`](#config-priority): boolean | `true`
  - [`ExcludeEndpoints`](#config-excludeendpoints): list of strings | no default

</Tab>

<Tab heading="YAML" group="yaml">

- [`apiVersion`](#apiversion): string | required | must be set to `consul.hashicorp.com/v1alpha1`
- [`kind`](#kind): string | required | must be set to `RateLimit`
- [`metadata`](#metadata): map | required
  - [`name`](#metadata-name): string | required
- [`spec`](#spec): map | required
  - [`config`](#spec-config): map | required
    - [`readRate`](#spec-config-readrate): number | `100`
    - [`writeRate`](#spec-config-writerate): number | `100`
    - [`priority`](#spec-config-priority): boolean | `true`
    - [`excludeEndpoints`](#spec-config-excludeendpoints): map of strings | no default

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Complete configuration

When every field is defined, a rate limit configuration entry has the following form:

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="HCL" group="hcl">

```hcl
Kind                = "rate-limit"        # required
Name                = "global"            # required
config {                                  # required
  readRate          = 100 
  writeRate         = 100 
  priority          = true 
  excludeEndpoints  = ["<excluded.endpoint.1>", "<excluded.endpoint.2>", "..."] 
} 
```

</Tab>

<Tab heading="JSON" group="json">

```json
{
  "Kind": "rate-limit",                   // required
  "Name": "global",                       // required
  "Config": {                             // required
    "ReadRate": 100, 
    "WriteRate": 100,
    "Priority": true,
    "ExcludeEndpoints": [ "<excluded.endpoint.1>", "<excluded.endpoint.2>", "..." ]
  }
}
```

</Tab>

<Tab heading="YAML" group="yaml">

```yaml
apiVersion: consul.hashicorp.com/v1alpha1   # required
kind: RateLimit                             # required
metadata:
  name: global
spec:
  config:
    readRate: 100
    writeRate: 100
    priority: true
    excludeEndpoints:
      - "<excluded.endpoint.1>" 
      - "<excluded.endpoint.2>" 
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Specifications

This section provides details about the fields you can configure in the rete limit configuration entry.

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="HCL" group="hcl">

### `Kind`

Specifies the type of configuration entry to implement. Must be set to `rate-limit`.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: String value that must be set to `rate-limit`.

### `Name`

Specifies a name for the configuration entry that is used to identify the rate limit. Must be set to `global` to ensure only one active rate-limit configuration per cluster.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: String


### `Config`

Map that contains the details about the `rate-limit` configuration entry.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: List that can contain maps of the following parameters:
  - [`ReadRate`](#config-readrate)
  - [`WriteRate`](#config-writerate)
  - [`Priority`](#config-priority)
  - [`ExcludeEndpoints`](#config-excludeendpoints)

### `Config[].ReadRate`

Specifies the read limit applied to the cluster.

#### Values

- Default: `100`
- Data type: Number

### `Config[].WriteRate`

Specifies the write limit applied to the cluster.

#### Values

- Default: `100`
- Data type: Number

### `Config[].Priority`

Specifies if the rate limit must be enforced by the cluster. The limit is enforced only if priority flag is enabled. If the priority flag is false, then only other existing server-wide rate limiter are enforced: [read/write server rate limits](/consul/docs/manage/rate-limit) or [IP-based rate limits](/consul/docs/manage/rate-limit/source). 

The config entry limit is evaluated first and enforces limits only when priority flag is enabled. Priority endpoints like health checks always bypass rate limiting. 

#### Values

- Default: `true`
- Data type: boolean

### `Config[].ExcludeEndpoints`

Specifies an optional list of endpoints that will be excluded from the rate limit configuration.

#### Values

- Default: None
- Data type: List of strings

</Tab>

<Tab heading="YAML" group="yaml">

### `apiVersion`

Specifies the version of the Consul API for integrating with Kubernetes. The value must be `consul.hashicorp.com/v1alpha1`.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- String value that must be set to `consul.hashicorp.com/v1alpha1`.

### `kind`

Specifies the type of configuration entry to implement. Must be set to `RateLimit`.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: String value that must be set to `RateLimit`.

### `metadata`

Map that contains an arbitrary name for the configuration entry.

#### Values

- Default: None
- Data type: Map

### `metadata.name`

Specifies a name for the configuration entry that is used to identify the rate limit. Must be set to `global` to ensure only one active rate-limit configuration per cluster.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: String

### `spec`

Map that contains the details about the `RateLimit` configuration entry. The `apiVersion`, `kind`, and `metadata` fields are siblings of the spec field. All other configurations are children.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: Map

### `spec.config`

Map that contains the details about the `rate-limit` configuration entry.

#### Values

- Default: None
- This field is required.
- Data type: Map

### `spec.config.readRate`

Specifies the read limit applied to the cluster.

#### Values

- Default: `100`
- Data type: Number

### `spec.config.writeRate`

Specifies the write limit applied to the cluster.

#### Values

- Default: `100`
- Data type: Number

### `spec.config.priority`

Specifies if the rate limit must be enforced by the cluster. The limit is enforced only if priority flag is enabled. If the priority flag is false, then only other existing server-wide rate limiter are enforced: [read/write server rate limits](/consul/docs/manage/rate-limit) or [IP-based rate limits](/consul/docs/manage/rate-limit/source). 

The config entry limit is evaluated first and enforces limits only when priority flag is enabled. Priority endpoints like health checks always bypass rate limiting. 

#### Values

- Default: `true`
- Data type: boolean

### `spec.config.excludeEndpoints`

Specifies an optional list of endpoints that will be excluded from the rate limit configuration.

#### Values

- Default: None
- Data type: List of strings

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Examples

The following examples demonstrate common rate limit configuration patterns for specific use cases.

### Apply a global rate limit to all endpoints excluding the health checks

In the following example, the configuration entry defines a that applies a global rate limit of 200 write requests and 300 read requests. 

<Tabs>

<Tab heading="HCL" group="hcl">

```hcl
Kind                = "rate-limit"        # required
Name                = "global"            # required
config {                                  # required
  readRate          = 300 
  writeRate         = 200 
  priority          = true 
} 
```

</Tab>

<Tab heading="JSON" group="json">

```json
{
  "Kind": "rate-limit",                   // required
  "Name": "global",                       // required
  "Config": {                             // required
    "ReadRate": 300, 
    "WriteRate": 200,
    "Priority": true
  }
}
```

</Tab>

<Tab heading="YAML" group="yaml">

```yaml
apiVersion: consul.hashicorp.com/v1alpha1   # required
kind: RateLimit                             # required
metadata:
  name: global
spec:
  config:
    readRate: 300
    writeRate: 200
    priority: true
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>
